hypothesis: An adaptive dynamic batching algorithm that updates node/edge padding targets online using an exponential moving maximum of observed graph sizes will reduce JAX gradient-update recompilations by at least 50% and lower mean combined training time per step by at least 20% while producing final test RMSE indistinguishable (p>0.10) from the original dynamic and static-64 algorithms on SchNet, MPEU, and PaiNN trained on QM9 and AFLOW.
